name: kind (Kubernetes in Docker)

performance.notes: Cluster creation ~30-120s; lightweight for CI environments

agent_readiness.notes: Kubernetes clusters using Docker containers as nodes. kind create cluster. kind create cluster --name my-cluster --config kind-config.yaml. Multi-node: config with role: control-plane + multiple role: worker nodes. Load images: kind load docker-image my-image. Ingress support (extraPortMappings). Kubeconfig: kind get kubeconfig. Destroy: kind delete cluster. Standard for CI Kubernetes testing (GitHub Actions). Lighter than minikube. Official Kubernetes project.

gotchas: Requires Docker (not Podman without additional config)
No persistent storage across cluster recreations
LoadBalancer type services don't work without additional tools (MetalLB)
Docker image loads required (images don't pull from host Docker cache automatically)
best_when: GitHub Actions / CI Kubernetes integration testing
Testing Kubernetes manifests, operators, and CRDs locally
Multi-node cluster testing without VM overhead
avoid_when: Long-lived development clusters (use minikube or OrbStack k8s)
When persistent storage required across restarts
summary: kind (Kubernetes in Docker) creates real Kubernetes clusters using Docker containers as nodes. kind create cluster. Multi-node with config YAML. Standard for CI Kubernetes testing (GitHub Actions). kind load docker-image to use local images. Fast cluster creation (~1-2min). Official kubernetes-sigs project. Lighter than minikube for CI. LoadBalancer and persistent storage need workarounds.
